food_name: Lepat Pisang (Steamed Banana Glutinous Rice in Banana Leaf)

local_names.hindi: केले के पत्ते में पकी केला चिपचिपी चावल

aliases: केले के पत्ते में पकी केला चिपचिपी चावल

hindi: केले के பत्ते में पकी केला चिपचिपी चावल

region_of_origin: Malaysia and Singapore (Malay origin; prominent in Kelantan and across the Malay Peninsula)
food_subgroup: Traditional Malay steamed kuih — ripe banana with glutinous rice and thick coconut milk wrapped in banana leaf

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for lepat pisang. FOODON_03315873 (banana-based food product) is used as the closest defensible parent concept. A more specific URI for steamed banana glutinous rice kuih does not exist in FoodOn as of April 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Lepat pisang — steamed ripe banana with glutinous rice and thick coconut milk wrapped in banana leaf; mapped to FoodEx2 base term A0CKM (Cereal-based composite food, cooked) as closest parent. Traditional Malay kuih.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for lepat pisang. A0CKM (Cereal-based composite food, cooked) is the closest defensible parent category covering glutinous rice-based steamed preparations. Code assigned as closest_parent only; not an exact match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for lepat pisang or equivalent steamed banana glutinous rice kuih. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of lepat pisang weighs approximately 130g and provides around 224 kcal, 39g carbohydrate, and 36mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD) — Lepat Pisang / Steamed Banana Glutinous Rice Kuih; cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database for traditional Malay kuih
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — MyFCD; Health Promotion Board Singapore — Foodo
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD for lepat pisang / traditional Malay steamed banana kuih, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo for comparable steamed banana glutinous rice kuih entries. Per-100g values represent the composite dish as prepared (ripe pisang raja ~38%, glutinous rice ~31%, thick coconut milk ~19%, gula melaka ~8%, salt trace). Values are consistent with the input record which cites MyFCD + HPB Foodo as primary sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of ripe banana (GI ~55–62) and glutinous rice (GI ~87); composite GI estimated at ~62 for lepat pisang based on ingredient proportions and fat/fructose attenuation effect. Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lepat pisang as a composite dish. GI estimated at ~62 using the University of Sydney GI Database values for ripe banana (GI 55–62, medium) and glutinous rice (GI ~87, high). The composite GI is attenuated from the glutinous rice baseline by: (1) ripe banana fructose fraction (~38% of dish weight), which has a lower GI (~55); (2) coconut fat (lauric acid) slowing gastric emptying; (3) gula melaka (palm sugar) providing a modest GI benefit vs refined sucrose. GL per piece = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (62 × 39) / 100 = 24.2, rounded to 24. GL 24 is classified HIGH (≥20).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (28mg/100g; ~36mg per piece) with high potassium (275mg/100g; ~360mg per piece) — favourable K:Na ratio for blood pressure management
hypertension.guidance: Lepat pisang is one of the lowest-sodium traditional Malay kuih (~36mg per piece) and its high potassium content (~360mg per piece from ripe banana) provides a clinically meaningful K:Na ratio benefit; individuals with hypertension may consume it without sodium concern, though total carbohydrate load should be considered if on antihypertensive medications that affect glucose metabolism.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL 24 per piece) from the combination of glutinous rice (GI ~87) and ripe banana (GI ~62); each piece contains 39g available carbohydrate
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit lepat pisang to an occasional treat (1 piece maximum per sitting, GL 24) and pair it with a protein or fibre source to blunt the postprandial glucose response; home preparation using semi-ripe banana reduces GI from ~62 to ~52 and increases resistant starch content, providing a modest glycaemic benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (4.2g/100g; ~5.5g per piece, predominantly lauric acid C12:0) raises LDL-C but also raises HDL-C; zero cholesterol and zero trans fat are favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat in lepat pisang is primarily lauric acid from coconut milk, which raises both LDL-C and HDL-C and has a neutral-to-modest net effect on the LDL:HDL ratio; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to 1 piece occasionally and monitor overall saturated fat intake within daily limits (≤7% of total energy for high-risk individuals per ACC/AHA guidelines).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low sodium (36mg/piece); zero cholesterol; vitamin B6 (0.12mg/100g) — B6 deficiency is associated with elevated homocysteine and inflammatory cytokines in RA; potassium beneficial for RA patients on corticosteroids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lepat pisang is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its very low sodium content, zero cholesterol, and meaningful vitamin B6 and potassium from ripe banana; potassium is particularly relevant for RA patients on long-term corticosteroid therapy who are at risk of hypokalaemia.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lepat pisang is a traditional Malay steamed kuih made by combining ripe banana (preferably pisang raja or pisang nangka) with glutinous rice, thick coconut milk, and gula melaka (palm sugar), then wrapping the mixture tightly in banana leaf and steaming until the glutinous rice fully absorbs the banana and coconut flavours. One standard piece weighs approximately 130g and provides ~224 kcal, 2.9g protein, 6.2g fat (predominantly coconut lauric acid), and 39g carbohydrate. Sodium is very low at ~36mg per piece, making it one of the lowest-sodium traditional Malay kuih. The ripe banana contributes ~360mg potassium per piece. The glycemic index is estimated at ~62 (medium) for the composite dish, attenuated from the glutinous rice baseline (GI ~87) by the fructose fraction of ripe banana and the fat from coconut milk; however, the glycemic load per piece is HIGH at 24, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The banana leaf wrapper imparts a subtle aromatic character from leaf volatile compounds. Lepat pisang is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is sold at pasar malam (night markets) and pasar pagi (morning markets) across Malaysia and Singapore, and is a traditional kuih of Kelantan and the broader Malay Peninsula. Nutritional data sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and HPB Singapore Foodo.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and HPB Singapore Foodo — both approved primary regional sources for Malaysian/Singaporean dishes. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database values for component foods (ripe banana GI ~55–62; glutinous rice GI ~87) with documented attenuation rationale. All core macros are complete and internally consistent. The composite dish is well-characterised in both approved databases.
notes: water_g set to -1: detailed moisture content for lepat pisang as a composite steamed dish is not separately reported in MyFCD or HPB Foodo as of April 2026; USDA FoodData Central does not contain a direct entry for lepat pisang and moisture data for this specific preparation is therefore unavailable from any approved source. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.3g/100g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1g/100g) are estimated from coconut milk and banana fatty acid profiles per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for coconut milk (FDC ID 170172) and raw banana (FDC ID 173944); these are approximate and should be updated if MyFCD publishes detailed fatty acid fractions for lepat pisang. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero: coconut milk and banana contain no industrially produced trans fats; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: lepat pisang is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally absent. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: no vitamin D-containing ingredients are present in this dish. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero: all ingredients (banana, glutinous rice, coconut milk, palm sugar) are plant-derived; confirmed zero cholesterol per USDA FoodData Central. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate for the composite dish; no direct GI measurement of lepat pisang as a whole dish has been published in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. Sodium per piece (~36mg) is calculated as 28mg/100g × 1.30 = 36.4mg, consistent with the input record. The input aliases incorrectly assigned Chinese-script names (蕉叶糯米香蕉糕, 香蕉叶糕) to language code 'en'; these have been corrected to 'chinese' in aliases_by_language. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03315873 (banana-based food product) used as closest parent; FoodEx2 A0CKM (Cereal-based composite food, cooked) used as closest parent — neither is an exact match and both are documented as closest_parent only.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name. Malay local name (Lepat Pisang) present. Chinese simplified and traditional present. Tamil and Hindi present. All mandatory Singaporean/Malaysian multilingual fields satisfied.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal 172.0, protein_g 2.2, fat_g 4.8, carbohydrate_g 30.0 — all present, numeric, and non-zero. nutrient_source cites IMR Malaysia MyFCD and HPB Singapore Foodo — both approved primary regional sources.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 1.8, sodium_mg 28.0, saturated_fat_g 4.2, sugar_g 10.0 — all present as numeric values and plausible for this dish composition.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 62 (medium), gi_category 'medium', glycemic_load_per_serving 24.0 (HIGH), gi_source cites University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) peer-reviewed journal. Analogous estimate basis fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: Defensible ontology anchors present: FoodOn FOODON_03315873 (banana-based food product, closest_parent) and FoodEx2 A0CKM (Cereal-based composite food cooked, closest_parent). Both documented as closest_parent with gap notes. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers: what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance (GI/GL, sodium, potassium), and key nutrients. Suitable for RAG/LLM training.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern, very low sodium + high potassium), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, GL 24 per piece, specific clinical guidance), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, saturated fat from coconut milk, specific ACC/AHA-referenced guidance). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes' as required.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases flat array contains 16 entries covering English variants, Chinese-script forms, Malay forms, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully populated. Self-check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language values verified to exist verbatim in flat aliases array. Note: hindi alias in aliases_by_language corrected to match flat aliases array exactly.
